hacker_news.author: theaniketgiri
hacker_news.summary: AIP-1 is a trustless, cross-platform protocol for verifying the identity, intent, and authorization boundaries of autonomous AI agents before they act. Think of it as OAuth + TLS, purpose-built fo...
comment_highlights:
Hi HN — I'm Aniket, working on AIP.

As AI agents begin executing real-world actions (calling APIs, writing to databases, triggering workflows), there's currently no native identity or authorization layer for them.

AIP is an open protocol that gives every agent a cryptographic identity and req...

linked_context.page_title: GitHub - theaniketgiri/aip: AIP-1 is a trustless, cross-platform protocol for verifying the identity, intent, and authorization boundaries of autonomous AI agents before they act. Think of it as OAuth + TLS, purpose-built for the agentic...
linked_context.page_description: AIP-1 is a trustless, cross-platform protocol for verifying the identity, intent, and authorization boundaries of autonomous AI agents before they act. Think of it as OAuth + TLS, purpose-built fo...
linked_context.page_excerpt:
The HTTPS for AI Agents. Cryptographic identity, intent verification, and boundary enforcement for autonomous agents.

Every AI framework lets agents do things . None of them verify what agents are allowed to do .

A LangChain agent can drain a bank account. An AutoGPT agent can email your customers. A CrewAI agent can delete production data. There is no standard way to verify an agent's identity, enforce its boundaries, or revoke it in real-time.

hacker_news.title: Show HN: Architecture question: running an LLM as core infrastructure
hacker_news.author: senza1dio
hacker_news.summary: Assistente AI specializzata in elettrovalvole, oleodinamica e automazione industriale.

linked_context.page_title: Liara AI — Assistente Tecnico | Zeli srl
linked_context.page_description: Assistente AI specializzata in elettrovalvole, oleodinamica e automazione industriale.
linked_context.page_excerpt:
Elettrovalvole, oleodinamica, automazione industriale. Chiedimi quello che vuoi.

Liara e sotto continuo training e le risposte potrebbero essere non corrette. Per maggiori dettagli chiamare Zeli srl 059/361320

hacker_news.content_context:
I've been experimenting with running an LLM not as a chatbot but as the core runtime of a business system, and I'm curious how others approach this.

The idea is that the model doesn't just answer questions but orchestrates tools and interacts with real application logic.

The architecture I'm currently testing includes:

Runtime

tool orchestration parallel tool execution loop detection circuit breaker / timeout guards token budgeting Context

context compression dynamic token ceiling Caching

deterministic LLM response cache semantic cache using pgvector Memory

short-term session memory longer-term semantic memory Evaluation

prompt evaluation set to test tool reasoning and failures I'm trying to figure out which parts are actually necessary in production and which ones are over-engineering.

For people building LLM systems beyond simple chat interfaces:

how do you handle tool orchestration? do you implement memory layers or just rely on context? are semantic caches worth it in practice? Curious to hear how others structure this. Assistente AI specializzata in elettrovalvole, oleodinamica e automazione industriale. Elettrovalvole, oleodinamica, automazione industriale. Chiedimi quello che vuoi. Liara e sotto continuo training e le risposte potrebbero essere non corrette. Per maggiori dettagli chiamare Zeli srl 059/361320

hacker_news.title: Show HN: Auto-Save Claude Code Sessions to GitHub Projects
hacker_news.author: ej31
hacker_news.summary: Auto-save every Claude Code session to GitHub Projects. Never lose Prompt & Context again. - ej31/claude-session-tracker

linked_context.page_title: GitHub - ej31/claude-session-tracker: Auto-save every Claude Code session to GitHub Projects. Never lose Prompt & Context again.
linked_context.page_description: Auto-save every Claude Code session to GitHub Projects. Never lose Prompt & Context again. - ej31/claude-session-tracker
linked_context.page_excerpt:
Every prompt, every response, every decision — automatically saved to GitHub Projects.

Quick Start • What It Does • How It Works • Use Cases • Configuration

One command, done. A private GitHub repo gets created, a Project board gets wired up, and every Claude Code session you run from now on is automatically logged as a GitHub Issue — prompts, responses, timestamps, everything.

hacker_news.content_context:
I wanted a way to preserve Claude Code sessions. Once a session ends, the conversation is gone — no searchable history, no way to trace back why a decision was made in a specific PR.

The idea is simple: one GitHub Issue per session, automatically linked to a GitHub Projects board. Every prompt and response gets logged as issue comments with timestamps. Since issues can reference PRs, you get full context tracing between conversations and actual code changes.

npx claude-session-tracker

The installer handles everything: creates a private repo, sets up a Projects board with status fields, and installs Claude Code hooks globally. It requires gh CLI — if missing, the installer detects and walks you through setup.

Why GitHub, not Notion/Linear/Plane?

I actually built integrations for all three first. Linking sessions back to PRs was never smooth on any of them, but the real dealbreaker was API rate limits. This fires on every single prompt and response — essentially a timeline — so rate limits meant silently dropped entries. I shipped all three, hit the same wall each time, and ended up ripping them all out. GitHub Issues via the gh CLI against your own repo has no practical... Auto-save every Claude Code session to GitHub Projects. Never lose Prompt & Context again. - ej31/claude-session-tracker Every prompt, every response, every decision — automatically saved to GitHub Projects. Quick Start • What It Does • How It Works • Use Cases • Configuration One command, done. A private GitHub repo gets created, a Project board gets wired up, and every Claude Code session...

hacker_news.title: Kraken – open-source autonomous dev agent for the terminal
hacker_news.author: galfrevn
hacker_news.content_context:
Hey HN,

I've been building Kraken for the past month — an open source autonomous dev agent that runs entirely in your terminal.

The architecture is a three-process system: a Rust scheduler (cron + file watchers), a Go LLM gateway (supports OpenAI, Anthropic and OpenRouter), and a TypeScript/React TUI built with OpenTUI. All three communicate over ConnectRPC on localhost.

A few things I wanted to get right from the start:

- Model-agnostic: uses a custom XML-based tool-calling protocol instead of native provider APIs, so it works the same regardless of the LLM - Plugin system: plugins implement a KrakenPlugin interface from the SDK, can register tools, hook into the agent lifecycle and extend the system prompt - No cloud: all state lives in a local SQLite file

It's early and a lot is still missing (docs, tests, CI). I'm sharing it now because I'd rather get feedback from people who know what they're doing than polish it in private.

Repo: github.com/galfrevn/kraken

Happy to answer questions about the architecture or design decisions.

hacker_news.title: Can Car-T – A "Living Drug" – Cure Autoimmune Diseases?
hacker_news.author: bookofjoe
hacker_news.summary: A “living drug” was developed as a cancer treatment. Now it is showing promise for conditions, such as lupus and multiple sclerosis, that have long been considered incurable.

linked_context.page_title: Could a Cancer Treatment Cure Autoimmune Diseases?
linked_context.page_description: A “living drug” was developed as a cancer treatment. Now it is showing promise for conditions, such as lupus and multiple sclerosis, that have long been considered incurable.
linked_context.page_excerpt:
When Talaya Reid was in high school, in a quiet suburb of Philadelphia, she developed fatigue so severe that she spent afternoons napping instead of going out with friends. She was lethargic at school and her grades suffered, but after graduation she enrolled in community college, where she did well enough to transfer to Temple University. Then, in the summer of 2017, when Reid was twenty-one,...

Reid didn’t notice any other symptoms until, a few months later, she awoke with a searing pain in her abdomen. She drove to the E.R., where doctors found protein in her urine—a sign that lupus was affecting her kidneys. She spent eleven days in the hospital being pumped full of medications; by the time she was ready to go home, her body was engorged with twenty pounds of extra fluid, a result o...

Lupus, which afflicts some five million people worldwide, is a leading cause of death among young women. Doctors had little to offer Reid beyond steroids, which reduce inflammation but can cause...

The US economy expanded an annualized 0.7% in Q4 2025, the weakest performance since Q1 2025 and well below 1.4% in the advance estimate.

Consumer spending slowed more than anticipated (2% vs 2.4% in the advance estimate), as purchases of both goods (0.4%) and services (2.7%) cooled.

Fixed investment also increased less than expected (1.6% vs 2.6%), largely due to a sharper drop in structures (-7.1%) while investment in equipment (3.9%) and intellectual property products (5.7%) remained robust.

The US economy expanded an annualized 0.7% in Q4 2025, the weakest performance since Q1 2025 and well below 1.4% in the advance estimate. Consumer spending slowed more than anticipated (2% vs 2.4% in the advance estimate), as purchases of both goods (0.4%) and services (2.7%) cooled. Fixed investment also increased less than expected (1.6% vs 2.6%), largely due to a sharper drop in structures (-7.1%) while investment in equipment (3.9%) and intellectual property products (5.7%) remained robust.

hacker_news.title: 'The munchies' are real and could benefit those with no appetite
hacker_news.author: PaulHoule
hacker_news.summary: The urgent onset of "the munchies" after cannabis use isn't imaginary—it's a cognitive response that occurs regardless of sex, age, weight or recent food consumption and could offer clues to help people struggling with appetite loss. Those findings from a collaborative study, conducted by researchers at Washington State University and the University of Calgary, were recently published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences.

linked_context.page_title: 'The munchies' are real and could benefit those with no appetite

linked_context.page_excerpt:
This article has been reviewed according to Science X's editorial process and policies . Editors have highlighted the following attributes while ensuring the content's credibility:

The urgent onset of "the munchies" after cannabis use isn't imaginary—it's a cognitive response that occurs regardless of sex, age, weight or recent food consumption and could offer clues to help people struggling with appetite loss. Those findings from a collaborative study, conducted by researchers at Washington State University and the University of Calgary, were recently published in the Pr...

"There are a lot of different diseases, conditions and disorders associated with wasting syndromes and lack of appetite, and this study really supports the idea that cannabis can be used medicinally to increase appetite in people who have conditions like HIV, AIDS, or who are on chemotherapy," said Carrie Cuttler, a WSU psychology professor.

hacker_news.title: The Environment Is Greater Than the Will
hacker_news.author: momentmaker
hacker_news.summary: On resonance, pilgrimage routes, and the emptiness where bliss lives

linked_context.page_title: The Environment Is Greater Than the Will
linked_context.page_description: On resonance, pilgrimage routes, and the emptiness where bliss lives
linked_context.page_excerpt:
On resonance, pilgrimage routes, and the emptiness where bliss lives

You notice it before you understand it. Somewhere around the third day, maybe the fourth — when the blisters have settled into a dull fact and the mind has stopped composing the email it will send when this is over — something shifts. Not in you. Around you.

The air has a different weight. The path, which looked like any other dirt track on a satellite image, feels held. The trees along this particular stretch have been leaning over walkers for eight hundred years. The stones beneath your boots were smoothed by feet that were not yours.

hacker_news.content_context:
On resonance, pilgrimage routes, and the emptiness where bliss lives

On resonance, pilgrimage routes, and the emptiness where bliss lives You notice it before you understand it. Somewhere around the third day, maybe the fourth — when the blisters have settled into a dull fact and the mind has stopped composing the email it will send when this is over — something shifts. Not in you. Around you. The air has a different weight. The path, which looked like any other dirt track on a satellite image, feels held. The trees along this particular stretch have been leaning over walkers for eight hundred years. The stones beneath your boots were smoothed by feet that were not yours.

AIP is an open protocol that gives every agent a cryptographic identity and req...

linked_context.page_title: GitHub - theaniketgiri/aip: AIP-1 is a trustless, cross-platform protocol for verifying the identity, intent, and authorization boundaries of autonomous AI agents before they act. Think of it as OAuth + TLS, purpose-built for the agentic...
linked_context.page_description: AIP-1 is a trustless, cross-platform protocol for verifying the identity, intent, and authorization boundaries of autonomous AI agents before they act. Think of it as OAuth + TLS, purpose-built fo...
linked_context.page_excerpt:
The HTTPS for AI Agents. Cryptographic identity, intent verification, and boundary enforcement for autonomous agents.

Every AI framework lets agents do things . None of them verify what agents are allowed to do .

A LangChain agent can drain a bank account. An AutoGPT agent can email your customers. A CrewAI agent can delete production data. There is no standard way to verify an agent's identity, enforce its boundaries, or revoke it in real-time.

top_trends.title: Show HN: Architecture question: running an LLM as core infrastructure
top_trends.author: senza1dio
top_trends.summary: Assistente AI specializzata in elettrovalvole, oleodinamica e automazione industriale.

linked_context.page_title: Liara AI — Assistente Tecnico | Zeli srl
linked_context.page_description: Assistente AI specializzata in elettrovalvole, oleodinamica e automazione industriale.
linked_context.page_excerpt:
Elettrovalvole, oleodinamica, automazione industriale. Chiedimi quello che vuoi.

Liara e sotto continuo training e le risposte potrebbero essere non corrette. Per maggiori dettagli chiamare Zeli srl 059/361320

top_trends.content_context:
I've been experimenting with running an LLM not as a chatbot but as the core runtime of a business system, and I'm curious how others approach this.

The idea is that the model doesn't just answer questions but orchestrates tools and interacts with real application logic.

The architecture I'm currently testing includes:

Runtime

tool orchestration parallel tool execution loop detection circuit breaker / timeout guards token budgeting Context

context compression dynamic token ceiling Caching

deterministic LLM response cache semantic cache using pgvector Memory

short-term session memory longer-term semantic memory Evaluation

prompt evaluation set to test tool reasoning and failures I'm trying to figure out which parts are actually necessary in production and which ones are over-engineering.

For people building LLM systems beyond simple chat interfaces:

how do you handle tool orchestration? do you implement memory layers or just rely on context? are semantic caches worth it in practice? Curious to hear how others structure this. Assistente AI specializzata in elettrovalvole, oleodinamica e automazione industriale. Elettrovalvole, oleodinamica, automazione industriale. Chiedimi quello che vuoi. Liara e sotto continuo training e le risposte potrebbero essere non corrette. Per maggiori dettagli chiamare Zeli srl 059/361320

top_trends.title: Show HN: Auto-Save Claude Code Sessions to GitHub Projects
top_trends.author: ej31
top_trends.summary: Auto-save every Claude Code session to GitHub Projects. Never lose Prompt & Context again. - ej31/claude-session-tracker

linked_context.page_title: GitHub - ej31/claude-session-tracker: Auto-save every Claude Code session to GitHub Projects. Never lose Prompt & Context again.
linked_context.page_description: Auto-save every Claude Code session to GitHub Projects. Never lose Prompt & Context again. - ej31/claude-session-tracker
linked_context.page_excerpt:
Every prompt, every response, every decision — automatically saved to GitHub Projects.

Quick Start • What It Does • How It Works • Use Cases • Configuration

One command, done. A private GitHub repo gets created, a Project board gets wired up, and every Claude Code session you run from now on is automatically logged as a GitHub Issue — prompts, responses, timestamps, everything.

top_trends.content_context:
I wanted a way to preserve Claude Code sessions. Once a session ends, the conversation is gone — no searchable history, no way to trace back why a decision was made in a specific PR.

The idea is simple: one GitHub Issue per session, automatically linked to a GitHub Projects board. Every prompt and response gets logged as issue comments with timestamps. Since issues can reference PRs, you get full context tracing between conversations and actual code changes.

npx claude-session-tracker

The installer handles everything: creates a private repo, sets up a Projects board with status fields, and installs Claude Code hooks globally. It requires gh CLI — if missing, the installer detects and walks you through setup.

Why GitHub, not Notion/Linear/Plane?

I actually built integrations for all three first. Linking sessions back to PRs was never smooth on any of them, but the real dealbreaker was API rate limits. This fires on every single prompt and response — essentially a timeline — so rate limits meant silently dropped entries. I shipped all three, hit the same wall each time, and ended up ripping them all out. GitHub Issues via the gh CLI against your own repo has no practical... Auto-save every Claude Code session to GitHub Projects. Never lose Prompt & Context again. - ej31/claude-session-tracker Every prompt, every response, every decision — automatically saved to GitHub Projects. Quick Start • What It Does • How It Works • Use Cases • Configuration One command, done. A private GitHub repo gets created, a Project board gets wired up, and every Claude Code session...

top_trends.title: Kraken – open-source autonomous dev agent for the terminal
top_trends.author: galfrevn
top_trends.content_context:
Hey HN,

I've been building Kraken for the past month — an open source autonomous dev agent that runs entirely in your terminal.

The architecture is a three-process system: a Rust scheduler (cron + file watchers), a Go LLM gateway (supports OpenAI, Anthropic and OpenRouter), and a TypeScript/React TUI built with OpenTUI. All three communicate over ConnectRPC on localhost.

A few things I wanted to get right from the start:

- Model-agnostic: uses a custom XML-based tool-calling protocol instead of native provider APIs, so it works the same regardless of the LLM - Plugin system: plugins implement a KrakenPlugin interface from the SDK, can register tools, hook into the agent lifecycle and extend the system prompt - No cloud: all state lives in a local SQLite file

It's early and a lot is still missing (docs, tests, CI). I'm sharing it now because I'd rather get feedback from people who know what they're doing than polish it in private.

Repo: github.com/galfrevn/kraken

Happy to answer questions about the architecture or design decisions.

source_briefs.item_name: Show HN: AIP – A Cryptographic Identity Protocol for Autonomous AI Agents
source_briefs.content_summary: AIP-1 是一个无信任、跨平台的协议，旨在自主AI代理执行操作前，验证其身份、意图和授权边界。作者指出，当前AI代理框架（如LangChain、AutoGPT、CrewAI）缺乏原生的身份验证和授权层，使得代理可能执行危险操作（如清空银行账户、删除生产数据）。该协议旨在为每个代理提供加密身份，并强制执行其操作边界，类似于为AI代理设计的OAuth + TLS。
source_briefs.ai_comment: 信号：针对AI代理安全性的标准化协议提案，直击当前代理框架缺乏授权层的核心痛点。噪音：项目处于早期阶段，实际采纳和效果有待验证。重要性：随着AI代理执行更多现实世界操作，身份与授权成为关键基础设施问题。

source_briefs.item_name: Show HN: Architecture question: running an LLM as core infrastructure
source_briefs.content_summary: 作者探讨将大语言模型（LLM）作为业务系统核心运行时（而非仅聊天机器人）的架构设计。其测试架构包括运行时（工具编排、并行执行、循环检测、断路器）、上下文管理、缓存（确定性缓存、语义缓存）、内存（短期会话记忆、长期语义记忆）和评估系统。作者询问在超越简单聊天界面的LLM系统中，哪些组件是生产环境必需的，哪些是过度设计，并具体探讨工具编排、内存层和语义缓存的实践价值。
source_briefs.ai_comment: 信号：关于LLM作为核心系统运行时的深入架构讨论，反映了AI应用从辅助工具向自主决策引擎的演进。噪音：描述偏重技术清单，缺乏具体性能数据或案例研究。重要性：为构建复杂、可靠的AI驱动系统提供了实用的工程思考框架。

source_briefs.item_name: Trump says U.S. struck Kharg Island, core of Iran’s oil economy - The Washington Post
source_briefs.content_summary: 据《华盛顿邮报》报道，特朗普宣称美国打击了伊朗石油经济的核心——哈尔克岛。相关报道汇总显示，德黑兰方面称美国从阿联酋发动了攻击，而伊朗战争已进入第三周。同时，特朗普声称伊朗“已被彻底击败”，并有多篇报道聚焦于哈尔克岛的战略意义及战争的最新进展。
source_briefs.ai_comment: 信号：涉及重大地缘政治事件，直接影响全球能源市场与安全格局。噪音：信息源自政治声明和媒体报道，需谨慎对待具体作战细节和战果宣称。重要性：此类事件对油价、供应链及国际关系有即时且深远的影响，是宏观风险分析的关键变量。
